lede: A look at October 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including "Weird Al" Yankovic and Pelé, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Israel
events.desc: Israel and the Palestinian Authority sign the Wye River Memorandum.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: The 1st Cavalry Division (Airmobile), in conjunction with the Army of the Republic of Vietnam, launches an operation seeking to destroy Communist forces during the siege of Plei Me.

events.title: Belgium
events.desc: Belgian artist Peyo's comic characters, the Smurfs, make their debut in Spirou magazine.

events.title: Hungarian Revolution of 1956
events.desc: Secret police shoot several anti-communist protesters, igniting the Hungarian Revolution.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Allied forces commence the Second Battle of El Alamein, which proves to be the key turning point in the North African campaign.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler and Francisco Franco meet at Hendaye to discuss the possibility of Spain entering the Second World War.

events.title: Alberto Santos-Dumont
events.desc: Alberto Santos-Dumont flies an airplane in the first heavier-than-air flight in Europe.

events.title: National Women's Rights Convention
events.desc: The first National Women's Rights Convention begins in Worcester, Massachusetts.

events.title: Battle of Grathe Heath
events.desc: The Battle of Grathe Heath ends the Danish Civil War.

births.name: "Weird Al" Yankovic
births.line: American comedy musician, writer, and actor

deaths.text: Soong Mei-ling, Chinese wife of Chiang Kai-shek, 2nd First Lady of the Republic of China

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast of the Most Holy Redeemer
Christian feast day: James the Just (i.e. James, brother of Jesus) (Lutheran, Episcopal Church (USA), Eastern Orthodox)
Christian feast day: Amon of Toul (Diocese of Toul)
